message:
Fix 'Failed to load dashboard' toast when switching between dashboards (#4011)

The KeepAlive outlet keeps the main Dashboard component mounted (hidden)
when the user navigates to other routes.  The loadDashboard effect
depended on location.key, which changes on every navigation — not just
when returning to "/".  This caused a background API call to fire while
the dashboard was invisible, and any failure (timeout, 404, invalid
JSON) displayed a confusing error toast.

Three fixes applied:

1. Guard the loadDashboard effect with a pathname check so it only
   fires when the home dashboard is the active route.
2. Guard data.cards access against undefined/null to prevent TypeError
   on malformed API responses.
3. Suppress error toasts during background refreshes — only show them
   for explicit foreground loads the user triggered.
4. Add 'Request timeout', 'API error:', and 'Invalid JSON' to the
   expected-failure list in both Dashboard and CustomDashboard so
   transient backend issues don't surface as user-facing errors.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -324,10 +324,13 @@ export function CustomDashboard() {
       const isExpectedFailure = error instanceof BackendUnavailableError ||
         error instanceof UnauthenticatedError ||
         (error instanceof Error && (
+          error.message.includes('Request timeout') ||
           error.message.includes('Failed to fetch') ||
           error.message.includes('NetworkError') ||
           error.message.includes('Load failed') ||
-          error.message.includes('HTTP request to an HTTPS server')
+          error.message.includes('HTTP request to an HTTPS server') ||
+          error.message.includes('API error:') ||
+          error.message.includes('Invalid JSON')
         ))
       if (!isExpectedFailure) {
         console.error('Failed to load dashboard:', error)
